label: Decoder-only causal transformers

node.label: Decoder-only causal transformers
node.definition: A decoder-only transformer predicts each next token from a causally masked context, enabling scalable autoregressive pretraining, continuation, and instruction following.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Decoder-only causal transformers is modeled as depending on Transformers, Causal, masked & structured masking and feeding downstream nodes such as Retrieval-augmented generation, Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Program synthesis & code search.
examples: atlas downstream link to Retrieval-augmented generation
atlas downstream link to Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning
atlas downstream link to Program synthesis & code search
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Transformers, Causal, masked & structured masking.

dependencies.rationale: Decoder-only causal transformers extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
Decoder-only causal transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Causal, masked & structured masking.
In this atlas graph, Decoder-only causal transformers has a `composes` dependency on Autoregressive decoding with `common` strength.

In this atlas graph, Decoder-only causal transformers has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Autoregressive / next-step prediction because Autoregressive / next-step prediction is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
